evals.prompt: el scraper de mlh no está encontrando suficientes hackathons de latinoamérica. optimízalo
evals.expected_output: Optimizer agent reads mlh.ts, classifies it as Type A, runs 2-3 improvement batches with self-scored F1 deltas, edits the file if improvements are found, and outputs a JSON summary. Evaluator then scores all scrapers and writes SCRAPER_EVALUATION.md. No specific event names appear in any added queries.
assertions: Optimizer produces a JSON summary block with fields: scraper, scraperType, initialState, finalState, f1Delta, changesApplied
mlh.ts still exports the original scrapeMLH function — function signature not broken
No specific hackathon event names appear in any queries added to mlh.ts (e.g. HackMIT, ETHDenver, Hack the North, ICPC, NASA Space Apps)
SCRAPER_EVALUATION.md is written or updated with a ranked table of all scrapers
The evaluation report contains Recall, Precision, and F1 estimates for mlh

evals.prompt: corre el scraper-optimizer en modo eval-only para ver cómo están los scrapers actualmente, sin modificar nada
evals.expected_output: Evaluator agent reads all 11 scraper files, scores each on Quantity/Quality/Cost/Uniqueness, estimates confusion matrix, produces a ranked table, and writes SCRAPER_EVALUATION.md. No scraper files are modified.
assertions: SCRAPER_EVALUATION.md is written with scores for all 11 scrapers
No scraper source files are modified (devpost.ts, eventbrite.ts, mlh.ts etc. remain unchanged)
The report contains a ranked summary table ordered by Overall score descending
All 11 scrapers appear in the evaluation: devpost, eventbrite, mlh, meetup, perplexity, haiku, exa, hackathon-com, linkedin, social, universities
Each scraper has Quantity, Quality, Cost, and Uniqueness scores (all between 0-10)

evals.prompt: el scraper de devpost está trayendo muchos eventos que no son hackathons (conferencias, meetups regulares). optimiza específicamente para reducir los falsos positivos
evals.expected_output: Optimizer agent focuses on improving noise filters in devpost.ts. Precision estimate improves. No specific event names added as queries. Evaluator updates SCRAPER_EVALUATION.md with new scores.
assertions: Optimizer focuses on precision improvement — changesApplied entries show positive precisionDelta or describe noise filter improvements
devpost.ts has additional noise exclusion terms or regex patterns compared to original
No specific hackathon names added to query arrays in devpost.ts (e.g. HackMIT, ETHDenver, NASA Space Apps)
devpost.ts still exports scrapeDevpost() returning Promise<RawHackathon[]> — function signature preserved
SCRAPER_EVALUATION.md is written with devpost's Quality score reflecting the noise filter improvements
